description:
fz_print_i64(n: i64) already exists in runtime/src/lib.rs. Wire it into runtime.fz:

1. Add extern declaration:
   extern "C" fn fz_print_i64(integer) :: nil

2. Add typed clause before the existing catch-all:
   fn print(x :: integer) do fz_print_i64(x) end

No C runtime changes needed. The fz-ty1.opt.1 singleton fold will prove the argument is integer at call sites like print(42), fold the TypeTest → Goto, and DCE will remove the dead boxing (ishl_imm / bor_imm). Bless add1/expected.clif after.

Tests: existing golden_clif tests cover the regression; interp tests cover correctness.
title: fz-ext.9: wire fz_print_i64 into runtime.fz for unboxed integer print
